topProblems.title: Hard to find a clean, reliable, and affordable ride in a timely manner
Street-hailing and phone dispatch are opaque — unknown ETA, cash hassle, no trip trail
Drivers lack flexible demand and spend too much time idle or chasing radio dispatch

existingAlternatives.title: Other ride-hailing (later: Lyft and local clones)

features.title: Mobile app that matches riders with nearby drivers in minutes
Cashless card payment with automatic receipts
Live GPS tracking, ETAs, trip history, and two-sided ratings

targetUsers.title: Urban dwellers who need fast, dependable trips across the city
Business travelers who want a professional, on-demand car
Night-out riders when transit is sparse or unreliable

earlyAdopters.title: Frequent taxi users unhappy with service

statements.title: The smartest way to get around — tap a button, get a safe reliable ride in minutes
statements.detail: Single clear promise for riders: reliability and speed beat hailing uncertainty

highLevelConcepts.title: Personal driver in your pocket
highLevelConcepts.detail: X-for-Y: Uber = on-demand private car service via smartphone

paths.title: Word of mouth and rider referral codes
City launch ops — free rides at tech events and partnerships

expenses.title: App, web, SMS, and mapping infrastructure

returns.title: Commission take-rate on every completed trip

advantages.title: Two-sided network effects
advantages.detail: More riders attract more drivers (and vice versa) — hard to bootstrap once a city tips

advantages.title: Brand trust in ‘push-button reliable rides’
advantages.detail: Early SF wins compound into default mental model before clones arrive

advantages.title: City launch density playbook
advantages.detail: Neighborhood-by-neighborhood supply seeding is operational know-how, not just software
